high_consensus.description: Strong security ROI for minimal cost: switching to Unicode `\p{Cf}` closes clear bypass gaps in the same attack class with little regression risk.

disputed.description: The optimization is plausible but lower priority because `composeErrorBody` is likely stable and cheap; the added lazy indirection may not justify immediate churn without evidence of failures.
Programmatic character enumeration improves long-term test completeness and prevents silent coverage drift as the registry grows, with minimal complexity.
Expanding JSON envelope variants could improve robustness, but broad top-level key matching may increase false positives unless constrained by additional shape checks.

blockers.concern: Confusable-script bypass: NFKC normalization does not unify Cyrillic А (U+0410), Greek Α (U+0391), or other visually-identical glyphs with Latin A (U+0041). An attacker, a misbehaving upstream emitting localized error strings, or even a copy-paste-from-browser quirk could produce `АPI Error: 500 …` (with Cyrillic А) which would render IDENTICALLY in Discord but would NOT match any anchored ASCII-only regex in `RAW_API_ERROR_PATTERNS`. The triple-defense (trimStart + NFKC + zero-width strip) is incomplete against script-confusable substitution.
blockers.why_matters: The whole defense-in-depth claim of this PR rests on 'a raw API error CANNOT reach the wire'. A single Cyrillic codepoint defeats the entire substitution path while preserving visual identity — the user sees `АPI Error: 500 Internal Server Error` exactly as if the sanitizer never fired. This is exactly the failure class the PR was designed to prevent. Note: legitimate Anthropic/Bedrock SDKs always emit ASCII, so today this is theoretical, BUT (a) future SDK localizations, (b) error-message-passing through a translation/logging layer that 'helpfully' converts to typographic quotes/letters, and (c) test fixtures pasted from rich-text sources are realistic vectors. The SKP-001 fix in the PR addressed zero-width strip but did not address confusables.
blockers.recommendation: Apply a confusables-fold pass before regex matching. Two options: (1) use Unicode's `Confusables.txt` mapping (TR39) via a small precomputed lookup for the high-frequency confusable set (Cyrillic А/В/С/Е/Н/К/М/О/Р/Т/Х, Greek Α/Β/Ε/Ζ/Η/Ι/Κ/Μ/Ν/Ο/Ρ/Τ/Υ/Χ → Latin) covering the chars that actually appear in `API Error`/`Internal Server Error`/`bedrock`/`orchestrator`/etc.; (2) reject any probe whose Script property is not entirely Latin/Common before matching, substituting to errorTemplate on rejection. Option (1) is the lower-risk add. Add a regression test mirroring the existing zero-width-strip test suite with confusable variants of each pattern.

blockers.concern: Zero-width/control-character hardening is partial; only a narrow subset is stripped (`U+200B–U+200F`, `U+2060`, `U+FEFF`). Other invisible controls (for example bidi isolates `U+2066–U+2069`, `U+061C`, `U+00AD`) can still obfuscate the prefix and bypass anchored matching.
blockers.why_matters: A crafted or transformed upstream error string can evade sanitization and leak raw error material to Discord, directly violating the in-character-only invariant.
blockers.recommendation: Expand normalization to remove a broader, explicit set of Unicode format/control chars used for text obfuscation, and add regression tests for each added class.
